sanskrit: सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः । नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ॥
hindiTranslation: नित्य मेरेमें युक्त मनुष्य दृढ़व्रती होकर लगनपूर्वक साधनमें लगे हुए और भक्तिपूर्वक कीर्तन करते हुए तथा नमस्कार करते हुये निरन्तर मेरी उपासना करते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Ever united with Me, steadfast in vows, they worship Me by chanting, striving, and bowing with devotion.
essence: Steady devotion becomes a life of continual remembrance.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Devoted people keep chanting, bowing, and striving with steady commitment, always focused on Krishna.
simpleInsight: Love becomes a practice, not just a feeling.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna describes the people who stay inwardly connected to him. These devotees do not merely believe; they keep chanting, bowing, and practicing with firm resolve.
modernRelevance: You light a candle before dawn, repeat the same prayer, and keep showing up even when your mind is tired. Devotion lives in repetition.
translationLiteral: सततं (continuously) / कीर्तयन्तः (chanting) / मां (Me) / यतन्तः (striving) / च (and) / दृढव्रताः (steadfast in vows) / नमस्यन्तः (bowing) / च (and) / मां (Me) / भक्त्या (with devotion) / नित्ययुक्ताः (always united) / उपासते (worship)
insight: It shifts devotion from private emotion to repeated, embodied practice.
